title: Perfect Squares
description: Given a positive integer n, return the least number of perfect square numbers (for example, 1, 4, 9, 16, etc.) which sum to n.

examples.explanation: The perfect squares less than 12 are 1, 4, and 9. We can use 4 + 4 + 4 = 12, which uses three squares.
The perfect squares less than 13 are 1, 4, and 9. The best way to form 13 is 4 + 9, which uses two squares.
The only perfect square that sums to 1 is itself (1). Thus, it takes one perfect square.

constraints: Only positive integers will be provided as input.
hints: Consider using dynamic programming to build up the solution from smaller values.
You might want to precompute all the perfect squares up to n.
Think about how you can reduce the problem using previously computed results.
Consider if it's possible to reach n by subtracting a perfect square from it.
